tip: The Russian word for 'hi' is one of the first words every learner masters. Use it freely with friends.

words.notes: Use with friends, family, peers. Never with strangers or elders.
words.image_prompt: two cheerful friends waving hello to each other in a sunny park

tip: Russians take formality seriously. Using the wrong greeting can feel rude.

words.notes: Formal greeting. The first 'в' is silent.
words.image_prompt: a person bowing politely in a formal handshake at an office entrance
two business colleagues waving goodbye at a train station

tip: The two most powerful tiny words. Master these and you can survive any conversation.

words.image_prompt: a smiling person giving a thumbs up with a checkmark beside them

words.notes: Polite refusal. Stress on the only syllable.
words.image_prompt: a person gently shaking their head with a soft 'no' gesture

tip: These two words will instantly make Russians warm up to you.

words.notes: From 'спаси Бог' (God save). Stress on the second syllable.
words.image_prompt: two hands gently passing a small gift wrapped in ribbon
a waiter politely offering a cup of tea on a tray

tip: Both ways to apologize, but used differently — формал vs. casual.

words.sentence_en: Excuse me, where is the metro?
words.notes: Use to get attention or apologize politely.
words.image_prompt: a polite person tapping someone's shoulder to ask for directions

words.notes: Casual apology — to a friend, not a stranger.
words.image_prompt: a child holding flowers with an apologetic smile

tip: The standard follow-up after 'hi'. Practice this phrase until automatic.

words.image_prompt: two friends meeting on a bridge, one warmly asking how the other is doing
a smiling sun shining over a peaceful garden with flowers

tip: Russian has TWO 'you' — one for friends, one for elders. Mix them up and you'll seem cold or oddly informal.

words.image_prompt: a child pointing to themselves with a proud smile

words.notes: Use with friends, family, children only.
words.image_prompt: two close friends sitting on a sofa talking warmly

words.notes: Polite singular OR plural. Default for adults you don't know.
words.image_prompt: a young person respectfully addressing an elderly teacher

tip: Russian doesn't use 'to be' in present tense. 'He student' = 'He is a student'.

words.notes: Also used for masculine inanimate nouns ('it').
words.image_prompt: a young man waving from across a street with a friendly smile
a kind woman with a warm smile holding an open book

words.notes: No verb 'to be' in present tense.
words.image_prompt: a group of four friends linking arms and laughing together

words.notes: Used for all genders in plural.
words.image_prompt: a small crowd of diverse students walking together with backpacks

tip: Possessive words change shape based on what they modify. Don't stress, just notice the pattern.

words.notes: Feminine: моя. Neuter: моё. Plural: мои.
words.image_prompt: a hand gently holding a small house, claiming it as home
two hands exchanging a smartphone with a thoughtful gesture

tip: Russians love their parents. These words come up in every family conversation.

words.image_prompt: a loving mother hugging her child in a sunny kitchen
a father reading a bedtime story to his child

tip: Russian families are often close. Cousins and aunts are part of everyday life.

words.image_prompt: a large family of three generations gathered around a dinner table
two brothers playing with toy cars on the floor
two sisters sharing a colorful umbrella under gentle rain

tip: Russian grandparents (бабушка/дедушка) are warm, central figures — like in fairy tales.

words.notes: Famous in Russian culture as the cozy keeper of traditions.
words.image_prompt: a kind grandmother in a flowered apron baking pies in a cozy kitchen

words.notes: Often pictured with a white beard, telling stories.
words.image_prompt: a wise grandfather with a gentle smile holding a fishing rod by a lake

tip: Russian distinguishes male and female friends clearly. The gender matters.

words.image_prompt: two male friends sharing a meal at a casual cafe table

words.notes: Not romantic — just a female friend.
words.image_prompt: two female friends laughing together while walking with shopping bags

tip: Russian homes are warm refuges from cold winters. These words come up constantly.

words.image_prompt: a cozy wooden house with smoke rising from the chimney on a winter evening

words.notes: Most urban Russians live in apartments, not houses.
words.image_prompt: a bright modern apartment with plants and warm sunlight

tip: The kitchen (кухня) is the heart of any Russian home — where tea and stories happen.

words.image_prompt: a sunny child's bedroom with toys and a small bookshelf

words.sentence_en: We drink tea in the kitchen.
words.image_prompt: a warm rustic kitchen with a steaming kettle and bread on the table

tip: Pay attention to gender endings: -ь often means feminine, even on inanimate things.

words.notes: Neuter nouns typically end in -о or -е.
words.image_prompt: an open window with curtains gently fluttering, view of a garden outside
an old wooden door with a heart-shaped knocker, slightly ajar

tip: These words appear in nearly every sentence about daily life.

words.sentence_en: The book is on the table.
words.image_prompt: a wooden table with a vase of wildflowers and an open book

words.notes: Don't confuse with English 'stool' — this is any chair.
words.image_prompt: a comfortable wooden chair with a soft cushion by a sunny window

tip: Russian literature is world-famous. Books are deeply respected in this culture.

words.image_prompt: an open antique book with golden pages and a feather quill beside it
a beautiful blue fountain pen resting on a handwritten letter

tip: Many tech words in Russian are borrowed from English — easy wins for learners!

words.image_prompt: a modern smartphone resting on a wooden table with a coffee cup
a vintage laptop computer open on a cozy desk with a steaming mug

tip: Bread (хлеб) is sacred in Russian culture. There's even a saying: 'хлеб — всему голова' (bread is the head of everything).

words.image_prompt: a clear glass of water with water droplets, set on a wooden table
a fresh round loaf of dark Russian bread sliced on a wooden board

tip: Russians drink TEA, not coffee, traditionally. A samovar (large tea urn) is iconic.

words.image_prompt: a traditional Russian samovar with a steaming glass cup of tea

words.notes: Often added by the cube, traditionally.
words.image_prompt: a small bowl of white sugar cubes beside a teacup

tip: Coffee (кофе) is masculine but indeclinable — it never changes form. A small grammar gift.

words.image_prompt: a steaming cup of dark coffee with rising vapor on a cafe table
a glass bottle of fresh milk beside a small jug, soft morning light

tip: Apples and bananas — easy starter fruits with simple pronunciations.

words.image_prompt: a glossy red apple with a green leaf on a white plate
a bunch of three ripe yellow bananas with a leaf

tip: Russian cuisine traditionally features lots of meat, especially in winter.

words.image_prompt: a hearty plate of grilled meat with vegetables and herbs
a fresh whole fish on ice with lemon and dill garnish

tip: Borscht is the most famous Russian soup — beet-based and ruby-red.

words.notes: Borscht is a famous Russian суп.
words.image_prompt: a bowl of red borscht soup with a dollop of sour cream and dill

words.notes: Olivier salad is a Russian classic.
words.image_prompt: a fresh garden salad with tomatoes and cucumbers in a wooden bowl

tip: Russians have a serious sweet tooth — desserts are part of every gathering.

words.image_prompt: a slice of beautiful chocolate layer cake with berries on top
a colorful pile of wrapped candies and chocolates in a tin

tip: Russian's biggest gift to learners: NO 'is/am/are' in present tense. 'Я студент' = 'I student' = 'I am a student'.

words.sentence_en: I want to be a doctor.
words.notes: Omitted in present tense! 'Я студент' = 'I am a student'.
words.image_prompt: a person looking thoughtfully at their reflection in a mirror

tip: Russians don't say 'I have'. Instead, they say 'by me there is' (у меня есть). Strange but melodic.

words.notes: Я живу, ты живёшь, он живёт.
words.image_prompt: a person opening curtains to a sunny morning, embracing a new day

words.notes: Russians don't have 'have' — they 'have by them'.
words.image_prompt: a smiling person holding a small kitten with love

tip: Russian has SEPARATE verbs for walking vs. riding. Don't mix them up!

words.image_prompt: a person walking down a quiet street with a backpack
a person looking out of a train window watching the countryside pass

tip: Russian conjugation has patterns — once you learn one verb, many others follow the same rules.

words.notes: Я читаю, ты читаешь, он читает.
words.image_prompt: a person curled up in a cozy chair absorbed in reading a book

words.notes: Я пишу, ты пишешь, он пишет.
words.image_prompt: a hand writing a letter with a fountain pen at a desk

tip: These two will be your most-used verbs as a beginner. Practice them daily.

words.notes: Я говорю, ты говоришь, он говорит.
words.image_prompt: two people having an animated conversation with speech bubbles

words.notes: The most useful phrase for beginners!
words.image_prompt: a person with a thoughtful expression having a lightbulb moment

tip: These modal verbs unlock the ability to make polite requests in Russian.

words.notes: Irregular: я хочу, ты хочешь, он хочет.
words.image_prompt: a child looking longingly at a cake in a bakery window

words.notes: Я могу, ты можешь, он может.
words.image_prompt: a person extending a helping hand to lift another up a hill

tip: Russian has TWO 'to know' verbs — one for facts, one for people. Today's is for facts.

words.notes: For facts. Different from знакомиться (to meet/know a person).
words.image_prompt: a person studying with books, a small lightbulb glowing above them
a person looking dreamily out a window with thought clouds above

tip: Adjectives in Russian agree with the noun's gender. Masculine ends in -ый, feminine in -ая, neuter in -ое.

words.image_prompt: a thumbs up with sparkles and warm golden light
dark clouds and rain over a small house with sad mood

tip: Russia is the largest country in the world (большая страна) — big is a big deal!

words.image_prompt: a small person looking up at an enormous oak tree
a tiny kitten sleeping curled up in a teacup

tip: Russians have deep respect for things 'old' — старый often implies wise, not outdated.

words.image_prompt: a shiny new gift box with a red ribbon being opened

words.notes: Old can be wise or shabby — context matters.
words.image_prompt: a charming old wooden house with weathered shutters and a garden

tip: These are compliments Russians love to give. Use them liberally.

words.image_prompt: a stunning sunset over a peaceful lake with reflections
a curious child examining an interesting insect with a magnifying glass

tip: Russian uses different cases for time. 'In the morning' = утром (with -ом ending).

words.image_prompt: a bright sun shining over rolling green hills at noon
a starry night sky over a quiet village with a glowing moon

tip: 'Доброе утро' (good morning) and 'Добрый вечер' (good evening) are essential daily greetings.

words.image_prompt: morning sunlight streaming through a window onto a breakfast table
warm orange evening light over a cobblestone city street with lamps

tip: Watch out for 'сегодня' — the 'г' is pronounced as 'в'! A Russian quirk.

words.image_prompt: a calendar with today's date circled, surrounded by sunshine
a sunrise over mountains symbolizing a new day ahead
an old photograph fading into memory with golden light

tip: The Russian week starts on MONDAY, not Sunday — like much of Europe.

words.image_prompt: a busy morning office scene with coffee and laptops on Monday
a notebook with Tuesday plans written, beside a coffee mug

tip: Среда (Wednesday) literally means 'middle' — middle of the week!

words.sentence_en: Wednesday is the middle of the week.
words.image_prompt: a hump-day cheerful image of Wednesday with a calm coffee break
a friendly business meeting with people gathered around a table
friends celebrating with drinks at the end of the work week

tip: Russians say 'выходные' for weekend — literally 'days of going out'.

words.image_prompt: a relaxed Saturday morning scene with someone reading in pajamas
a peaceful Sunday family brunch in a sunny kitchen

tip: Russians don't always raise their voice for questions. Intonation is subtle.

words.image_prompt: a silhouette behind frosted glass, mysterious and curious
a question mark floating over a mysterious wrapped present

tip: Master these and you can ask about anything in Russian.

words.image_prompt: a person looking at a map with a magnifying glass searching for a place
a clock face with hands pointing to question marks

tip: 'Как тебя зовут?' literally means 'how do they call you?' — a Russian curiosity.

words.image_prompt: a curious child tilting their head, surrounded by floating question marks

tip: Russian numbers change form depending on gender of what's being counted. Just memorize the basic forms first.

words.notes: Changes form by gender: одна (f), одно (n).
words.image_prompt: a single lit candle on a wooden table

three colorful balloons floating against a blue sky

tip: Notice пять (5) ends in soft sign — this triggers a tricky case change in Russian counting.

words.image_prompt: four seasons illustrated as a square: spring, summer, fall, winter

tip: After 5, all numbers trigger the genitive plural in Russian — pure grammar trivia for now, just enjoy the words!

words.notes: Both s and m are soft.
words.image_prompt: seven colorful umbrellas in a row, like a rainbow

ten gold coins stacked neatly in a small pile

tip: Russia (Россия) is the world's largest country, spanning 11 time zones. Moscow is its heart.

words.image_prompt: Saint Basil's Cathedral on a snowy Red Square at golden hour
the Kremlin towers against a colorful Moscow sunset

tip: When traveling in Russia, knowing 'where' (где) something is becomes essential.

tip: Most Russian cities have wide, tree-lined streets and beautiful old parks.

a sunlit park with flower beds and a wooden bench under trees

tip: Магазин is any 'store' — from grocery to electronics. Context tells you which.

a warm romantic restaurant interior with candles and wine glasses

tip: Russians take education seriously — universities are highly respected.

words.image_prompt: a charming brick schoolhouse with children walking in with backpacks

words.notes: Also means 'work' as in a piece of work.
words.image_prompt: an organized desk with laptop, coffee, and morning sunlight

tip: Russian emotions are often expressed through prefixes and verb pairs. Watch for patterns.

words.image_prompt: a person beaming with joy in a sunny meadow with flowers

tip: Russians speak about love in deep, philosophical ways. The word любовь carries centuries of poetry.

words.image_prompt: two hands forming a heart shape against a warm sunset sky

words.notes: Strong word — also for things you love.

tip: Emotional vocabulary unlocks deeper connections in any language.

words.sentence_en: Joy is in the small things.

tip: Russian literature is full of these tensions — fear and hope intertwined.

tip: Daily energy talk — use these to describe your state.

words.image_prompt: a person yawning and stretching after a long day of work

tip: Russia has a strong tradition of medicine and education — these professions are highly respected.

words.image_prompt: a kind doctor with a stethoscope smiling at a young patient

tip: Russia has world-renowned engineers AND artists. Pride in both!

tip: Russian universities have a rich tradition — being a student (студент) is a respected role.

tip: Russians use diminutives a lot for body parts — головка, носик, ручка — endearing forms.

tip: 'Глаз' has irregular plural — глаза (with stress on the second syllable).

tip: Russian uses 'рука' for both hand AND arm, 'нога' for both leg AND foot. No distinction!

words.image_prompt: two gentle hands holding a small bird carefully

tip: Russian culture cherishes 'soul' (душа) — it appears in countless songs and poems.

words.image_prompt: a glowing red heart shape made of soft watercolor
a glowing ethereal light rising from a person's chest

tip: Russian winters are brutal — clothing vocabulary becomes very important!

tip: Russian weather changes dramatically. Always be ready for anything!

tip: Russia is famous for its snow. Снег appears in countless folk tales.

tip: Russian cities have efficient public transport — and beautiful metros (especially Moscow's).

a person standing peacefully by a large window at sunset

tip: Body position verbs — very common but tricky conjugations.

words.image_prompt: a contented cat sitting on a windowsill in afternoon light

words.sentence_en: The book is lying on the table.
words.image_prompt: an open book lying on a wooden bench in a garden

tip: Essential transactional verbs — used dozens of times daily.

words.image_prompt: a hand gently offering a flower to another
a hand reaching out to take a fresh apple from a basket

tip: Russian comparatives are simpler than English — just add 'лучше' or 'хуже'.

words.image_prompt: two paths in a forest, one clearly easier and brighter
stormy weather darkening over previously sunny landscape

tip: Common adjective pair — describes physical and emotional states.

words.image_prompt: a person lifting a heavy stone with determination
a small candle flame flickering against a strong wind

tip: Useful for talking about Russian learning! Russian is hard, but rewarding.

words.image_prompt: a feather floating gently downward in soft light
a person climbing a steep but achievable mountain with effort

tip: These describe time, objects, distances — very versatile.

words.image_prompt: a long winding road stretching to the horizon
a short walking path leading to a small charming gate

tip: Russian cuisine has all five tastes — салат, борщ, varenye all explore these.

words.image_prompt: a beautiful pile of sweet pastries with honey dripping
a bright yellow lemon sliced open, juice glistening

tip: Critical modal-like words — they help you express opinions and obligations.

words.image_prompt: a glowing important letter sealed with red wax
an essential key hanging on a hook with golden glow

tip: Critical for discussions and statements — used constantly in Russian conversation.

words.image_prompt: a beam of pure white light illuminating an open hand
a tangled knot of red threads in shadow

tip: Modal expressions — they shape every Russian conversation.

words.image_prompt: an open door with warm light spilling into a darker space
a red stop sign on a clear background with simple iconography

tip: Душа is THE concept in Russian thought. Whole philosophy books are written on it.

words.image_prompt: a soft glow of inner light rising from a person's chest
a flame of inner light burning bright and warm

tip: Russian philosophy obsesses over смысл — the meaning of life, work, suffering.

words.image_prompt: a question mark made of glowing stars in the night sky

words.sentence_en: My goal is to learn Russian.
words.image_prompt: an arrow striking a target in soft golden light

tip: Свобода was a major theme of Russian thinkers — paired with its weight, ответственность.

words.image_prompt: a bird taking flight from an open cage into a sunlit sky
shoulders carrying a balanced weight of stones

tip: Honesty (честность) carries weight in Russian culture — it's a high virtue.

words.image_prompt: a balanced scale of justice with golden light
a clear glass heart radiating warm honest light

tip: Russian literature is haunted by memory — Pasternak, Akhmatova, Brodsky all wrote about it.

words.image_prompt: old photographs spread on a table with warm afternoon light
a faded photograph fading into mist and shadow

tip: Russian proverbs are full of wisdom themes — folk philosophy is everywhere.

words.image_prompt: an old wise owl perched on books in a moonlit library
a colorful court jester juggling badly with confetti

tip: Russians believe terпение (patience) is the greatest virtue. 'Терпение и труд всё перетрут.'

words.image_prompt: a gardener patiently tending to slow-growing plants
dark thunder clouds with lightning over mountains

tip: Russian war literature explores храбрость deeply — heroes and cowards in extreme conditions.

words.image_prompt: a knight in armor facing a dragon with sword raised
a small figure hiding behind a rock as shadows loom

tip: Dostoevsky famously claimed 'beauty will save the world' — а major Russian idea.

words.image_prompt: a stunning sunset over Saint Basil's Cathedral with vibrant colors
a withered dead tree alone in a barren landscape

tip: Russian literature deeply explores rich/poor divides — Tolstoy, Dostoevsky devoted novels to this.

words.image_prompt: a treasure chest overflowing with gold coins and gems
an empty bowl with one small bread crust in dim light

theme: Russian Idiom: Without a King in Head
tip: These idioms make you sound truly fluent — natural to native speakers.

words.meaning: Without a king in the head (= reckless)
words.image_prompt: an empty crown floating above an empty head silhouette

tip: Russians don't 'pull your leg' — they 'hang noodles on your ears'!

words.meaning: To hang noodles on someone's ears (= deceive)
words.image_prompt: a person whispering to another with noodles hanging from their ears

tip: Use this when you're too busy. Russians say it constantly.

words.meaning: Hands don't reach (= no time for)
words.sentence_en: I never get around to cleaning.
words.image_prompt: many hands reaching for too many tasks at once

theme: Russian Idiom: To Show Where Crayfish Hide
tip: Threatening phrase — but funny when you know the original meaning.

words.meaning: To show where crayfish spend winter (= threaten)
words.sentence_ru: Я ему покажу, где раки зимуют!
words.sentence_en: I'll show him a thing or two!
words.notes: Origin: where serfs hid crayfish for landlords.
words.image_prompt: a stern person pointing toward a frozen river with crayfish below

tip: Society words — useful for any newspaper article in Russian.

words.image_prompt: a diverse crowd of people gathered in a city square
a grand government building with classical columns at sunset

tip: Russian legal vocabulary — common in news, history, daily life.

words.image_prompt: an open law book with a gavel and scales beside it

words.notes: Standard Russian response to 'how are you?'.
words.image_prompt: neatly organized objects on a clean shelf in soft light

tip: Memory and dreams — Russian literature lives in these tenses.

words.image_prompt: an old sepia photograph fading into memory and warm light
a sunrise breaking over a hopeful horizon with new beginnings

tip: Two poles of Russian emotional vocabulary — both deeply explored in literature.

words.image_prompt: a single candle flame burning brightly in the dark
a figure sitting alone in heavy rain at twilight

tip: Strana, душа — these explore the Russian theme of meaningful suffering.

words.image_prompt: a person walking through a storm with quiet determination
people dancing joyfully around a bonfire at a festival

tip: Russian distinguishes ум (intellect) from разум (reason) — subtle but important.

words.image_prompt: a glowing brain icon surrounded by ideas and lightbulbs
a person thoughtfully balancing scales of logic and emotion

tip: Russian dreams (мечты) and reality (реальность) often contrast in poetry.

words.image_prompt: a person reaching up toward floating glowing dreams
a clear window between a dream and the real world outside

tip: Practical vocabulary for any conversation — basic but important.

words.image_prompt: a glowing question mark made of golden light
a lit lightbulb appearing above an open notebook

tip: Russian respects silence as much as words. 'Слово серебро, молчание золото.'

words.image_prompt: an old fountain pen writing the word 'слово' in elegant script
a quiet snowy landscape at dawn with no sound

tip: Russian intellectual vocabulary — for ideas, theories, mental life.

words.image_prompt: a glowing lightbulb floating against a dreamy night sky
thought clouds floating from a thinker's head in warm colors

tip: Russia's deep love of learning — and equally deep awareness of ignorance.

words.image_prompt: an open book with light beams emerging from its pages
a closed book covered in dust in a dark corner

tip: The most basic life vocabulary — uses up your concept of poetry.

words.image_prompt: a baby being gently held in soft warm light
a candle flame slowly extinguishing in still air

tip: Generations — a major theme in Russian family-focused stories.

words.image_prompt: a vintage scene of a child playing with toys in soft warm light
an elderly couple sitting peacefully on a bench at sunset

tip: Russian family vocabulary — common in social conversation.

words.image_prompt: a beautiful wedding scene with flowers and warm celebrations
two paths diverging with a single sad figure looking back

tip: Russian Orthodoxy shapes much of the language — even atheists carry the vocabulary.

words.image_prompt: an icon of light with golden rays in a peaceful church
a person standing at a crossroads with questioning expression

tip: Russians value tradition deeply — традиции shape everyday life.

words.image_prompt: a worn path through grass made by repeated footsteps
a multigenerational family lighting candles together for a tradition

tip: Religious vocabulary appears constantly in Russian — even in secular speech.

words.image_prompt: an old Orthodox church with golden onion domes at sunset
a collage of cultural symbols: book, art, music, dance

tip: Russians live with their history vividly — события (events) shape identity.

words.image_prompt: an ancient scroll unfurled showing layers of history
a horizon with sunrise breaking through fog into clarity

tip: These appear in countless Russian conversations about politics, life, character.

words.image_prompt: an open hand reaching up with golden light streaming through
a single small flame being protected from wind by cupped hands

tip: Russian distinguishes external and internal success — успех is more outward.

words.image_prompt: a person triumphantly standing atop a mountain peak
a fallen but still glowing leaf on the ground after a storm

tip: Heavy concepts — but they appear in Russian songs, novels, gangster films.

words.image_prompt: a handshake with golden light between two people
a broken chain or shattered glass in a dramatic shadow

tip: Friendship is deeply valued in Russian culture — friends are family.

words.image_prompt: two friends embracing warmly at a sunset with deep connection
a single tree standing alone in vast open landscape

tip: Russian distinguishes types of love — never just one word for everything.

words.image_prompt: two intertwined hands gently holding each other against sunset
two clashing storm clouds with dark electric energy

tip: Patriotic vocabulary — used in speeches, anthems, poetry.

words.image_prompt: a glowing flag against a sunset over an ancestral landscape
a soldier looking proudly at a landscape with a flag

tip: Pravда vs. истина — Russian distinguishes 'truth' as fact vs. truth as essence.

words.image_prompt: a beam of pure white light piercing through clouds
a magician's misdirection with mirrors and hidden hands

tip: Russian Orthodox imagery is rich — these concepts appear in art, idioms, blessings.

words.image_prompt: a heavenly garden with golden light and angelic peace
dark storm clouds with flames in the distance dramatically

tip: Совесть — the inner voice — is a major Russian concept, untranslatable.

words.image_prompt: a small bright light glowing inside an open heart shape
a person bowing their head with heavy shadow over them

tip: Self-sacrifice (самопожертвование) is the ultimate virtue in Russian moral philosophy.

words.image_prompt: a candle giving its last light willingly to warm others
an old golden medal on a velvet cushion with warm light

tip: Russian honor culture — честь is more important than money or comfort.

words.image_prompt: a knight's gleaming shield with engraved coat of arms
a person covering their face with hands in dim light

tip: Two virtues balanced — Russians know both can be excess.

words.image_prompt: a person standing tall with head held high in golden light
a quiet humble person tending to a small flower with care

tip: Russian has gorgeous idioms — once you master a few, you sound truly fluent.

words.meaning: To beat baklusha (= to be idle)
words.image_prompt: a person lazily watching clouds while ignoring their work

tip: Famous fable origin — a 'bear's service' is help that hurts more than it helps.

words.image_prompt: a bear awkwardly trying to help and accidentally breaking something

tip: Greek-origin idiom, adopted into Russian — used for sources of conflict.

words.meaning: Apple of discord (= source of conflict)
words.sentence_en: This is the source of conflict.
words.image_prompt: a single golden apple shining between two arguing figures

tip: Charming Russian idiom — 'so few that a cat cried' over it.

words.image_prompt: a small puddle of tears beside a single sad cat

theme: Idiom: To Carry Owls to Athens
tip: Russian way of saying 'unnecessary effort' — already plenty there.

words.meaning: To Tula with your own samovar (= bringing what's already plentiful)
words.sentence_ru: Это как в Тулу со своим самоваром.
words.image_prompt: a person carrying a samovar into a city of samovars

theme: Idiom: To Beat Around the Bush
tip: Russian phrasing — 'pour from empty to empty' — wasted talk.

words.meaning: To pour from empty to empty (= waste effort)
words.sentence_ru: Не переливай из пустого в порожнее.
words.image_prompt: an empty cup being poured into another empty cup repeatedly

tip: Russian distinguishes 'mood' (настроение) carefully — emotional weather of the soul.

words.image_prompt: a cheerful person enjoying a sunny morning with coffee
a warm cozy room with fireplace, books, and soft warm lighting

tip: Russian loves mysteries — тайна is core to Dostoevsky, detective novels, soul.

words.image_prompt: an old wooden treasure chest with a glowing keyhole
a hand pulling back a curtain to reveal sunlight and wonder

tip: Russian fatalism vs. free will — debated for centuries in literature.

words.image_prompt: a path winding through landscape with destiny shimmering ahead
two paths diverging in a forest, one bright, one shadowed

tip: Russian writers wrestle with the head-vs-heart conflict — endlessly.

words.image_prompt: a glowing heart with warm rays of light reaching out
a brain with gears and lightbulbs working in harmony

tip: Russian proverbs are gems — this one is essential for any learner.

words.word: Без труда не вытащишь рыбку из пруда
words.pronunciation: Byes tru-DA nye VY-ta-shchish RYB-ku iz pru-DA
words.ipa: /bʲɪs trʊˈda nʲe ˈvɨtəɕːɪʂ ˈrɨpkʊ ɪs prʊˈda/
words.meaning: Without work, you won't pull a fish from the pond (= no pain, no gain)
words.sentence_ru: Без труда не вытащишь рыбку из пруда!
words.image_prompt: a person patiently fishing in a peaceful pond at sunrise

tip: Beautiful proverb about appearances vs. reality — wisdom from the countryside.

words.meaning: In a quiet pond, devils live (= still waters run deep)
words.image_prompt: a perfectly still mysterious pond with strange shadows beneath

tip: On the power and danger of words — Russian wisdom never tires.

words.meaning: Your tongue will lead you to Kyiv (= asking helps you find anything)
words.sentence_en: If you ask, you'll find your way.
words.image_prompt: a tongue painted as a winding path leading to a distant city

tip: The most Russian of all proverbs — patience above all virtues.

words.meaning: Patience and work grind everything down (= persistence pays off)
words.image_prompt: a small but persistent stream wearing away a large stone

tip: Modern Russian absorbs English tech words rapidly. Often pronounced with Russian patterns.

words.image_prompt: a glowing world of connected lights and digital streams
futuristic glowing icons floating in modern minimalist space

tip: Modern communication vocabulary — useful for any 21st-century conversation.

words.image_prompt: an envelope icon glowing with a small notification dot
interconnected nodes of profile icons in a glowing network

tip: New Year (Новый год) is Russia's biggest holiday — bigger than Christmas. Stay tuned!

words.image_prompt: fireworks bursting over the Kremlin at midnight on New Year
champagne glasses being raised in celebration with warm lights

tip: A day to reflect on words you've learned so far.

words.notes: A day to thank yourself for committing to learning.
words.image_prompt: a peaceful sunset symbolizing gratitude and reflection
